page: Google Cloud Platform m4-megamem-28 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the m4 server family. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 28 dedicated vCPUs and 372.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of approximately 13.29 GB of RAM per core.

The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this server type.

Due to its high memory density, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It is suitable for large in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-bound data processing tasks that require substantial RAM relative to compute cores.
description: Google Cloud Platform m4-megamem-28 is a memory-optimized server from the m4 family, featuring 28 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture and 372.0 GB of RAM. This configuration delivers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 13.29 GB per core, making it suitable for memory-intensive enterprise applications. The server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage for data persistence. It is designed for workloads such as in-memory databases, large-scale data caching, and enterprise resource planning systems that demand substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources.
